title: Ambulance staff (excluding paramedics)
description: Assess injuries and illnesses and administer basic emergency medical care. May transport injured or sick persons to medical facilities. Administer basic or advanced emergency medical care and assess injuries and illnesses. May administer medication intravenously, use equipment such as EKGs, or administer advanced life support to sick or injured individuals. Supervise and coordinate the activities of ground crew in the loading, unloading, securing, and staging of aircraft cargo or baggage. May determine the quantity and orientation of cargo and compute aircraft center of gravity. May accompany aircraft as member of flight crew and monitor and handle cargo in flight, and assist and brief passengers on safety and emergency procedures. Includes loadmasters. (Based on 16 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Administer first aid treatment or life support care to sick or injured persons in prehospital settings, and perform emergency diagnostic and treatment procedures during ambulance rides.
Assess nature and extent of illness or injury to establish and prioritize medical procedures and observe, record, and report to physician the patient's condition or treatment provided.
Administer drugs, orally or by injection, and perform emergency pharmacological interventions and emergency invasive interventions before delivering patients to an acute care facility.
Coordinate work with other emergency medical team members, police, or fire department personnel, and communicate with dispatchers or treatment center personnel to arrange further treatment.
Comfort and reassure patients to alleviate anxiety during medical emergencies and while transporting them to medical facilities.
Attend training classes to maintain certification, keep abreast of new developments, and maintain knowledge of first-aid procedures.
Drive ambulances or assist ambulance drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons to specified locations.
Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water, and report any vehicle malfunctions or needed repairs.
Maintain vehicles and medical and communication equipment, and replenish first aid equipment and supplies to ensure readiness for emergencies.
Restraining or shackling violent patients as necessary to ensure safety during transport or treatment.
Prepare and maintain work records and reports of information such as employee time and wages, daily receipts, or inspection results.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers.
Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to telephone or radio reports of emergencies.
Sort materials, such as metals, glass, wood, paper, or plastics, into appropriate containers for recycling.
Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Record logs of recycled materials or waste chemicals removed from products.
Operate forklifts, pallet jacks, power lifts, or front-end loaders to load bales, bundles, or other heavy items onto trucks for shipping.
Check conditions and weights of vessels to ensure cleanliness and compliance with loading procedures.
